PreToolUse.matcher: gh pr create|gt submit|gt stack submit|but pr new

hooks.prompt: First run: git diff --name-only HEAD 2>/dev/null | grep '\.py$' to check if any Python files were modified. If the output is empty, immediately return {"decision": "allow"}. If Python files were changed: fetch the Python Design Principles Playbook at https://app.notion.com/p/3727d7f56298819cac31c4245e0870a9, read the False Positives section first, evaluate the changed Python files against principles P1-P17 using the scope rules in the playbook, apply the false positive filter. Return {"decision": "block", "reason": "VIOLATIONS: <summary>"} if any principles fail, or {"decision": "allow"} if all pass.

First run: git diff --name-only HEAD 2>/dev/null | grep '\.sql$' | grep -v 'dbt_packages/' to check if any project SQL files were modified. If the output is empty, immediately return {"decision": "allow"}. If SQL files were changed: fetch the PostgreSQL Design Principles Playbook at https://app.notion.com/p/3727d7f562988183b5d6e775ea065d30, read the False Positives section first, determine schema context (OLTP vs analytics — transform/dbt/ models are analytics: skip P1; tiny tables: P6-P8 advisory), evaluate the changed SQL against principles P1-P17, apply the false positive filter, and respect house conventions (singular table names, 30-char identifier soft cap). Return {"decision": "block", "reason": "VIOLATIONS: <summary>"} if any principles fail, or {"decision": "allow"} if all pass.
